name: North America
culture_notes: Visual Language: 'Anti-Ad' aesthetic. It shouldn't look like a high-budget commercial. Use 'POV' (Point of View) overlays and raw phone-captured textures.
Meme Sophistication: Move beyond static memes; use 'Reaction' style split-screens. One side is the gameplay, the other is a 'Person' (or AI persona) showing extreme confusion or smugness.
Diversity & Inclusivity: Visuals should reflect a multi-cultural society without being 'forced'. Realistic character proportions > stylized perfection.
The 'Choice' Mechanic: Use UI that mimics OS notifications or dating app swipes (Left/Right) to force a mental micro-decision in the first 2 seconds.
Fast-Cut ADHD Logic: Not just frame resets, but 'Zoom-ins' on specific UI numbers and 'Screen Shakes' on impact/failure.
creative_hooks: The 'Logic Trap': Use text like '99% of people pick the wrong door' or 'Are you actually smart? Prove it.' (Intellectual Ego Hook).
The 'Work vs. Life' relatable hook: Start with a scene of a boring office/boss yelling, then immediate transition to the 'Explosive' game world (Escape Reality).
The 'Nostalgia' Hook: Using 8-bit sound effects or UI elements that remind Gen X/Millennials of retro arcade games.
The 'ASMR/Satisfying' Hook: High-fidelity sounds of coins clinking, glass breaking, or grass cutting (The 'Brain Massage' effect).
preferred_bgm: Lo-fi Hip-hop for 'Chill' games; Phonk or High-energy Synthwave for Action/SLG. Avoid generic stock music.

tone_and_manner.voiceover: Use popular AI Text-to-Speech (TTS) voices like 'Jessie' (TikTok) or a sarcastic, deadpan male narrator. Sarcastic > Enthusiastic.
tone_and_manner.copywriting: Minimalist. Use slang like 'No Cap', 'POV', 'Bet', 'Level: Impossible'.

focus: Individual Agency (My choices matter) > Intellectual Superiority (I am smarter than the ad) > Instant Stress Relief
